hero.subtitleTop: Discover events. Meet people. Support local venues.

hero.commandTitle: Start with the room, the host, and the price signal.

hero.noteOneValue: Host-led formats and calmer arrival logic help solo attendees commit faster.
hero.noteTwoValue: Paid public inventory starts at 500 ISK so even smaller formats have a real commercial baseline.
hero.noteThreeValue: Partner rooms increase event quality, booking confidence, and local business value at the same time.
hero.noteFourValue: The best inventory clusters around social, expat, and practical workshop formats.

sections.thisWeekDescription: Curated events with venue-backed perks, friendly hosts, and enough texture to feel local.
sections.signalTrustDescription: Clear host identity, venue fit, and calmer arrival cues make the whole product feel more credible.

sections.signalVenueTitle: The city itself is a feature
sections.signalVenueDescription: Better rooms improve discovery, shape the event format, and create a stronger local loop.
sections.businessTitle: Designed for members, organizers, and venue partners at the same time.

sections.memberLaneDescription: Discovery is cleaner when people can judge the room, the host, and the format without guesswork.
sections.memberLanePointOne: Search-first browsing with visible trust signals
sections.memberLanePointTwo: Paid and free formats presented with clearer expectations
sections.memberLanePointThree: Group and venue context attached directly to each event
sections.organizerLaneDescription: Recurring hosts need more than a listing form. They need approvals, repeat formats, and revenue logic in the product itself.
sections.organizerLanePointOne: Public ticketing from 500 ISK and up
sections.organizerLanePointTwo: Recurring group identity and audience operations
sections.organizerLanePointThree: Venue matching and better commercial framing
sections.venueLaneDescription: Partner rooms are part of the operating system, not just passive listings on a directory page.
sections.venueLanePointTwo: Premium storefront presentation and event fit cues
sections.venueLanePointThree: Higher-value repeat business from better organizers
sections.flywheelTitle: A stronger local loop compounds across every page.
sections.flywheelDescription: The best version of this product is not just beautiful. It connects discovery, operations, and revenue clearly enough that each side of the marketplace reinforces the others.
sections.flywheelPointOneDescription: Members find formats faster when the product shows trust, price, venue, and host quality together.
sections.flywheelPointTwoDescription: Organizers and venues stay because the tooling feels like business infrastructure, not community hobbyware.
sections.flywheelPointThreeDescription: Paid seats, venue plans, and organizer subscriptions work better when the frontend already frames the value correctly.
sections.howTitle: A calmer way to discover the city
sections.howDescription: The product logic stays straightforward: browse, RSVP, and show up. No feed clutter, no endless swiping.

sections.venueAwareDescription: Featured venues, deals, availability, and admin-ready infrastructure for the later build phases.

hero.title: Clear data handling is part of trust, not a legal afterthought.
hero.description: This page covers the major policy surfaces the platform needs: account data, community activity, communications, analytics, and member rights.

identity.title: Identity and account data
identity.copy: The platform stores the profile, account, and role information needed to run memberships, organizer flows, and venue partnerships. That includes your public display name, your private email account, and the settings that control who can view your activity.
identity.point1: Profile visibility stays aligned with member privacy settings.
identity.point2: Organizer and venue accounts collect extra operational details for trust and review.
identity.point3: Admin access is reserved for moderation, support, and platform operations.

activity.title: Event, group, and venue activity
activity.copy: RSVPs, waitlist movement, approvals, attendance signals, and venue interactions are used to keep events reliable and to help organizers and admins make better decisions. The product is opinionated about trust and quality, so operational activity is part of the product record.
activity.point1: RSVP and approval history may be visible to organizers or admins where relevant.
activity.point2: Venue and event interactions can be used to improve recommendations and reduce abuse.
activity.point3: Payment and payout records are retained for billing and audit purposes.

comms.title: Communications and analytics
comms.copy: The platform sends transactional messages such as approvals, waitlist updates, reminders, and venue reviews. It also tracks aggregate analytics so the product can understand what formats, neighborhoods, and venue partnerships are working.
comms.point1: Digest and marketing preferences remain configurable in account settings.
comms.point2: Analytics are used for product improvement, not generic ad-network profiling.
comms.point3: Access, export, and deletion requests will be handled through admin review flows.

privacyPage.rightsDescription: The live admin and settings surfaces are already built to support these operations once backend wiring is connected.

access.title: Access your record
access.text: Profiles, RSVPs, memberships, and account settings can be surfaced for export.

correct.title: Correct profile data
correct.text: Members, organizers, and venues can update their operational identity over time.

billing.title: Billing and transaction review
billing.text: Once payments are live, the platform will retain the records needed for invoices and payouts.

locale.title: Bilingual policy surface
locale.text: Privacy and account controls are intended to work in both English and Icelandic.

hero.title: Clear rules for a curated city platform.
hero.description: These terms explain how membership, organizer activity, venue partnerships, moderation, and future payment flows are expected to work.

accounts.title: Accounts and membership
accounts.copy: Members, organizers, and venue partners use the same platform core, but each role carries different responsibilities. Accounts must use accurate identity and contact information, and the platform can restrict access when trust or safety signals require it.
accounts.point1: Members are responsible for profile accuracy and respectful participation.
accounts.point2: Organizers are responsible for truthful event listings and clear host behavior.
accounts.point3: Venue partners are responsible for accurate availability, deal, and booking information.

events.title: Events, payments, and cancellations
events.copy: Public events are designed to start at 500 ISK or higher unless they are sponsor-backed or specially approved. Booking holds, waitlists, and payment confirmations are part of the operating model. Fee handling, refunds, and payout timing follow the configured commercial terms.
events.point1: A confirmed seat or booking can depend on payment, approval, or both.
events.point2: Refund and cancellation treatment can differ by event, venue, or subscription state.
events.point3: Admin can intervene when quality, trust, or abuse issues affect a listing or transaction.

moderation.title: Moderation and platform control
moderation.copy: The product is intentionally curated. Admin may suspend, restrict, or remove users, groups, events, or venue listings when safety, trust, spam, harassment, or repeated quality failures justify intervention.
moderation.point1: Moderation actions can be temporary, permanent, or conditional on review.
moderation.point2: Organizer and venue visibility can be limited if quality or reliability drops.
moderation.point3: Appeals and support requests are reviewed through platform operations channels.

termsPage.stanceDescription: MeetupReykjavik is meant to feel intentional. That means admin, organizer, and venue actions are not purely mechanical.

trust.label: Trust layer
trust.value: Profiles, approvals, attendance, and moderation all matter.

rooms.label: Room quality
rooms.value: Admin and hosts may shape attendance to improve safety and fit.

providers.label: Providers
providers.value: Email and payments are added later without changing the core rules.

appeals.label: Appeals
appeals.value: Handled through support and moderation channels.

termsPage.remindersDescription: Key points to keep in mind while using MeetupReykjavik.

reminders.one: Your account, events, and community interactions are governed by these terms.
reminders.two: Organizers and venues operate within the guidelines outlined above.
reminders.three: Commercial terms, refund policies, and payout schedules follow the arrangements agreed with each provider.

hero.title: About MeetupReykjavik
hero.description: A local platform for events, groups, and venue partnerships — built specifically for Reykjavik.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.paragraph1: Reykjavik is small enough that quality matters fast. If events feel random, hosts feel weak, or the rooms do not fit the format, people notice immediately.
mission.paragraph2: We bring event context, group identity, and venue fit into one system. That is the difference between a feed and an actual local product.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.membersText: Find events, join groups, RSVP with confidence. Free to browse, with premium tiers for deeper access.
howItWorks.organizersText: Run recurring events with approval tools, paid ticketing, analytics, and venue matching built in.
howItWorks.venuesText: Partner with the platform to get bookings, host better community events, and offer member deals.

community.title: Community first
community.text: Every feature we build starts with the question: does this make real-world gatherings better?

trust.title: Trust by design
trust.text: Profiles, reviews, and approval tools so organizers, venues, and members feel safe and informed.

quality.title: Quality over quantity
quality.text: We reward strong hosts, well-matched rooms, and durable recurring communities — not vanity metrics.

local.title: Local context
local.text: Built for Reykjavik. Every venue, neighborhood, and format is shaped by the city itself.

cta.title: Join the Reykjavik community
cta.description: Whether you want to attend, organize, or host — there is a place for you.

hero.title: Frequently asked questions
hero.description: Quick answers about the platform, pricing, and how things work.

joiningProfiles.title: Joining and profiles
joiningProfiles.q1: Do I need to pay to browse or RSVP?
joiningProfiles.a1: No. Browsing and basic discovery are free. Paid features are only required for specific events or membership tiers.
joiningProfiles.a2: Profiles help organizers and venues understand their audience, and help members get clearer expectations around event format and fit.

eventsGroups.title: Events and groups
eventsGroups.q1: Why are some events manual approval and others open RSVP?
eventsGroups.a1: Both are supported. Approval works well when hosts want to shape the room, while open RSVP works for simpler, lower-friction formats.
eventsGroups.a2: Venue partners host events, offer member deals, and help shape the arrival experience. They are an active part of the platform.

trustSupport.title: Trust and support
trustSupport.a1: Use the contact page to reach the right team. We have separate channels for general help, organizer support, venue operations, and trust concerns.
trustSupport.a2: No. Messaging respects member privacy settings. Organizers can only reach members through approved channels.

faqPage.noResults: No questions match your search. Try different keywords.

cta.heading: Still have questions?
cta.description: Our team is here to help. Reach out and we will get back to you within one business day.

hero.title: Reach the right team without the usual support maze.
hero.description: Support, organizer operations, venue partnerships, and privacy questions each have a clear lane so the product stays helpful and accountable.
